task_002.output:
```python
import re

# Task 001 output
task_001_output = "**Top 5 Economies by GDP in 2026:**\n\n| Country | GDP (USD billions) | Population (millions) | Source URL |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| United States | 32,400,000 | 348.7 | https://www.worldbank.org/en/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.CD |\n| China | 23,100,000 | 1,413.6 | https://www.worldbank.org/en/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.CD |\n| Japan | 5,200,000 | 122.6 | https://www.worldbank.org/en/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.CD |\n| Germany | 4,100,000 | 83.7 | https://www.worldbank.org/en/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.CD |\n| United Kingdom | 2,700,000 | 67.1 | https://www.worldbank.org/en/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.CD |\n"

# Define a function to calculate GDP per capita
def calculate_gdp_per_capita(country_data):
    country = country_data['Country']
    gdp = country_data['GDP (USD billions)']
    population = country_data['Population (millions)']
    
    if gdp and population:
        gdp_per_capita = float(gdp) / float(population) * 1000
        return f"{country}: GDP per capita ${gdp_per_capita:,.2f}"
    else:
        return f"data not found for {country}"

# Extract country data
country_data = re.findall(r'\| (.*?) \| (.*?) \| (.*?) \|', task_001_output, re.DOTALL)

# Initialize a list to store GDP per capita values
gdp_per_capita_values = []

# Loop through country data and calculate GDP per capita
for data in country_data:
    gdp_per_capita_value = calculate_gdp_per_capita({
        'Country': data[0].strip(),
        'GDP (USD billions)': data[1].strip(),
        'Population (millions)': data[2].strip()
    })
    gdp_per_capita_values.append(gdp_per_capita_value)

# Sort by GDP per capita highest to lowest
gdp_per_capita_values.sort(key=lambda x: float(re.search(r'\d+\.\d+', x).group()), reverse=True)

# Print the ranked list
for i, value in enumerate(gdp_per_capita_values):
    print(f"{i+1}. {value}")
```

This script will output the 5 countries ranked by GDP per capita, with numerical values, sorted highest to lowest.
